stories.title: Thunder 139, Lakers 96 — SGA and OKC Roll to Blowout as No. 1 Seed Lead Grows

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma City 139. Los Angeles 96.

SGA had 28 points. The Thunder won by 43.

OKC is now 61-16. San Antonio is 3 games back. The Spurs are running out of time.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The OKC Thunder just blew out the Lakers by 43 points.

SGA: 28 points. Healthy. Locked in.

If you thought the No. 1 seed race was over before — the math is getting harder for San Antonio.

#HoopHeroes #SGA #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Thunder Crush Lakers 139-96 as SGA Stars in 43-Point Blowout

stories.title: Cavaliers Clinch Playoff Spot — Mitchell 25, Strus 24 in 118-111 Win Over Warriors

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
CLEVELAND IS IN.

Donovan Mitchell: 25 pts. Max Strus: 24 pts. Cavaliers beat the Warriors 118-111.

Cleveland officially clinched a 2026 playoff berth.

The East is nearly set.

#HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The Warriors lost to Cleveland 118-111 Thursday night — without Steph Curry.

Golden State is 36-40. The Play-In spot is not guaranteed. Curry better be back Sunday.

#HoopHeroes #Warriors #StephCurry

article.headline: Cavaliers Clinch 2026 Playoff Berth Behind Mitchell and Strus in 118-111 Win Over Warriors

stories.title: Pistons 113, Wolves 108 — Detroit Rallies in Q4 With Both Stars Out

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Detroit 113. Minnesota 108. Both Cade Cunningham AND Anthony Edwards were out.

Duren: 22/14. Jenkins: 26 pts. An 11-0 run in the 4th quarter to seal it.

This Pistons team is built different.

#HoopHeroes #Pistons #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Anthony Edwards and Cade Cunningham are now both INELIGIBLE for end-of-season awards.

Both are below the 65-game threshold.

Edwards (29.5 PPG). Cade (MVP watch before the injury).

The biggest individual awards story of the season.

#HoopHeroes #NBA #Awards

article.headline: Pistons Beat Wolves 113-108 Without Cade as Duren and Jenkins Shine; Edwards and Cunningham Award-Ineligible

stories.title: Curry Has 2nd 5-on-5 Scrimmage — Sunday Return vs. Houston Still the Target

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Steph Curry had his SECOND consecutive 5-on-5 scrimmage.

Kerr: "Everything went well. He looked good."

Sunday vs. Houston is still the target. The Warriors need this.

#HoopHeroes #StephCurry #Warriors

stories.title: Wembanyama Takes No. 1 on MVP Ladder — Historic Lines Fuel Voter Shift

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama just took over the No. 1 spot on the NBA MVP Ladder.

Youngest player EVER to post back-to-back 40+ pt / 15+ reb games.

Only player in 50 years with consecutive 40/15/3 lines.

Wemby is making his case.

#HoopHeroes #Wembanyama #MVP

article.headline: Wembanyama Takes No. 1 on MVP Ladder With Once-in-a-Generation Statistical Run

stories.title: Edwards and Cade Both Award-Ineligible — Star Injuries Reshape Accolades Season

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Anthony Edwards and Cade Cunningham are both officially ineligible for NBA awards.

Edwards: 29.5 PPG this season. Cade: was on MVP watch.

Two of the league's best young players — sidelined before they could make their full case.

#HoopHeroes #NBA #Awards

stories.title: East Playoff Picture — Detroit Magic Number 1; Cavs Clinched; Play-In Heats Up

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
East update (April 3):

Detroit: magic number 1 for No. 1 seed.
Cleveland: just clinched. ✓
Boston, New York: in. ✓
Next in line: Atlanta, Philadelphia, Orlando.

Regular season ends April 12.

#HoopHeroes #NBA #NBAPlayoffs
